description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.description: VERDICT CATEGORY OVERLOAD: 'Misleading' verdict conflates 4 distinct failure modes: (1) data misrepresentation, (2) duplication/redundancy, (3) non-discrimination (observation explained by multiple models equally), (4) circular derivation. 23 WINs under one label implies systematic dishonesty, but many are simply non-discriminating observations. Consider subcategory 'Non-Discriminating' or move non-discriminating WINs to 'Std Model Explains'. Affects verdict taxonomy coherence and reviewer's interpretation of dome author intent.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: Verdict taxonomy restructuring requires human editorial decision on subcategory definitions

issues.description: V51.1 split sun altitude: physical <4,200 km / apparent 5,733 km. Almost certainly responding to our sun/firmament collision critique. Our argument needs updating to show why the split makes things WORSE: (1) inequality is unfalsifiable, (2) refraction needed is ~15° vs real ~0.01°, (3) if refraction is that extreme, all triangulation measurements including the ones used to derive dome parameters become unreliable. Update the relevant kill-shot section.

V51.1 added Steelman Evidence Ladder (4 tiers) and killshot.html (August 2026 eclipse prediction). Evidence Ladder: Tier 1 (Australia) has 6.2% RMSE vs mm-level geodesy; none of the tiers discriminate dome from globe. Killshot page: Kp<2 precondition voids test ~50% of time; -28.9 nT conflicts with homepage -17 to -21 nT and code -29.1 nT (now THREE different values). Needs analysis section.
WIN-064 detail_evidence contains verbatim duplicate block (~700 chars): "On a flat disc 20,015 km in radius, there is no spherical liquid core..." through end appears twice in succession
WIN-065 detail_evidence: PRED-NEW-001 retrodiction argument appears twice — once concise, once expanded (different wording). Second occurrence should be removed.

WIN-065: Missing Oslo counterargument. Dome own coordinate table shows Oslo Polaris at 59.90° vs WGS84 59.91°N (0 excess), contradicting PRED-NEW-001 prediction of +4.5°. Only Chapel Hill of 24 cities shows any deviation.
WIN-062 detail_evidence: velocity computed as 472,400 km/s (should be 472,360) and percentage difference stated as 0.25% (should be 0.24%). Minor rounding error undermines precision-focused tone.
sections.json part7: P-wave shadow zone angular range stated as "140–103° from epicenter" — reversed and wrong inner boundary. Should be "104°–140°" (standard seismology notation, matches WIN-064).
